data.title: Unauthorized Aircraft Enters Restricted Airspace Over Mar-a-Lago
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, an unauthorized aircraft entered the restricted airspace over U.S. President Donald Trump's Mar-a-Lago estate at approximately 1:15 PM Eastern Time. The North American Aerospace Defense Command (NORAD) responded by dispatching fighter jets to escort the aircraft out of the area. During the interception, the fighter jets deployed flares to signal the pilot.

Gen. Gregory Guillot of the North American Aerospace Defense Command highlighted a rise in incidents involving violations of Temporary Flight Restrictions (TFR). He emphasized the importance of pilots checking Notices to Airmen (NOTAM) before flying, noting that over 20 similar incidents have occurred since President Trump took office.

data.title: Bitcoin Expected To Appreciate Amid Rising Adoption
data.description: According to BlockBeats, on March 10, Zach Pandl, Head of Research at Grayscale Investments, expressed confidence that Bitcoin can appreciate further this year without the need for U.S. strategic reserves. He anticipates that increased adoption will drive Bitcoin's price higher, with the cryptocurrency beginning to serve as a store of value. This is particularly relevant amid concerns that U.S. President Donald Trump's tariff policies could lead to renewed inflation. Despite these expectations, Bitcoin has recently been trading in sync with risk assets.
